title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons
summary: A look at the latest AI news, including OpenAI and Anthropic's push to prevent AI-developed biological weapons and a new server designed to break through AI's 'memory wall'.
imageAlt: Image from source article for OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons
imageCaption: OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons
imageSourceTitle: OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI brief. We're looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. I am looking at two fresh signals that caught my attention, and I'm excited to walk you through them.

First up, OpenAI and Anthropic, two major players in the AI space, have signed a letter urging Congress to adopt new laws that would prevent bad actors from developing biological weapons using their technology. This move is interesting because it highlights the growing concern about the potential misuse of AI. It's like the AI industry is acknowledging that it needs to be a responsible kid on the block.

Next, I'm looking at a new server that's trying to break through AI's 'memory wall'. Apparently, memory is a major constraint for large language models, and this server is designed to help overcome that limitation. My read is that this could be a game-changer for AI development, as it would allow models to process more information and potentially become even more accurate.

Also on the board is the ongoing challenge of data boundaries in AI. As models get smarter, they need more data to train on, but that data is often limited or hard to come by. It's a bit like trying to build a puzzle with missing pieces – you need to get creative with what you've got.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

New Server Hopes to Break Through AI’s “Memory Wall”
